title: Automation to have subitem dates xx days before item due date
excerpt: We have several use cases on templates where we want subitems to have due dates that precede the item due date. It would be great to have an automation that says “when item is created and date set, change subitem dates t…

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: We have several use cases on templates where we want subitems to have due dates that precede the item due date. It would be great to have an automation that says “when item is created and date set, change subitem dates to xx days/weeks before item date”. Otherwise, it’s always a manual process to fill these out each time, but it’s a constant process that subitems must always be completed the same day or week intervals in advance of an items due date.

posts.author: amichay (Amichay Even Chen)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @elizabeth.jaeger
You’ll be able to do that in our new workflow builder very soon!

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @elizabeth.jaeger

We developed an app called subitem automations that’s solves this use case.
You define the offset of each subitem from its parent item based on a numbers column (which you can keep hidden if needed) and there you have it →

Screenshot 2024-02-22 at 19.23.172038×402 42.9 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks this is helpful, but the added number column feature isn’t helpful for our use case. I’d love a mix of this with another automation you have "when subitem is created and item’s name is set its due date… If I could name the subitem, I’d love to be able to hardcode a number into the formula without having to create a new random column on the board.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Elizabeth,

2 questions to better understand your use case →

  1. All subitems will have the same due date (Relative to their parent item) or they differ between each other?
  2. Are the subitems always the same or change based on the item name?

If it’s better for you we can continue this discussion via email - daniel@fantasymedia.io

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: The subitems are the same every time on this board. The subitems have different due dates from each other, but all relate to the parent item. If I could use the “if name is” then I could hardcode the due date for each subitem, which will be the same every time.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: @elizabeth.jaeger got it. Will look into it and update you.

posts.author: Lewiss (SL)
posts.cookedHtml: This is going to save me Hours every week. Thank you!

posts.author: nmhanlon (Natalie Younger)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! We have a very similar use case. We have a set of 6 subitems that have different due dates, but all relate to a date column in the parent item. The pre-set automations in your app almost get us there, but not quite. We need the flexibility to do something like, "when subitem is created and subitem name is X, set date to [parent date column] plus X days/business days.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @elizabeth.jaeger & @nmhanlon - we’ll add it to the next version. Should be out next week.

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s amazing. Thank you! I look forward to having that rolled out.

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks again @Fantasy-Media-Team . Do you have an estimated date of when this update will be out?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)

We’re currently developing another app, once it’s done we’ll add this improvement. Should be about 1-2 weeks from now. Thanks for understanding!

posts.author: elizabeth.jaeger (Elizabeth)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi team, Thanks again for reaching out.
Unfortunately, this isn’t the automation I was looking for, but it’s really close. This matches the parent date exactly. I am looking for “When subitem is created and if the subitem’s name is this name set the value of its column to be +/- x days of column in its parent item.”

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @elizabeth.jaeger how about this one →

Screenshot 2024-05-08 at 14.49.392168×440 41.1 KB

posts.cookedHtml: Yes, thank you!

posts.author: ryan.anderson (Ryan Anderson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Fantasy-Media-Team - this app is really helpful, but I have a question. I’m using the automation from above that sets the subitems dates to be +/- x days of the parent date. However, my workflow also has an automation that is “when item is created, create 6 subitems.” All the subitems have a different name and due date (all in relation to the parent item date). So the order of operation is:

  1. create parent item
  2. Automation runs to create subitems
  3. Your app runs based on these subitems

The issue is on when I go to create my new item, it instantly starts creating the subitems before I have a chance to update the parent item date. And then since there is no parent date, your app doesn’t have a date to use to map the dates against. Is there a workaround to fix this?

Ideally, I need an app that basically combines my step 2 and 3 above. “When item is created, create a subitem called “xxxxxxx” and then set its date to match the parent item DATE +/- X days/weeks/months”. Does that exist or is it possible?

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Ryan,

What if you add something like “when item is created set date to today”? What is the item’s date? Do you know it in advance?

posts.author: patmandigital (Patrick Harman)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Fantasy-Media-Team what is the name column used in this example? I have tried to use this automation but it doesn’t fire when using the string that matches this column:

?

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@elizabeth.jaeger, hi! Yeah, that automation would save so much time - totally agree it should exist natively.
Until monday adds it, you could use Time in Status for monday.com to track how long each subitem stays open and whether it’s actually being completed ahead of the parent item’s due date. It’s not a date-setter, but it gives you solid visibility into timing and delays across your templates
